title: Real Estate Deals in Lakewood CO: A Complete Investor's Guide for 2024
excerpt: Lakewood, Colorado is one of the Denver metro's most overlooked markets for real estate investors — offering strong rental demand, flip opportunities, and wholesale deals just minutes from downtown. Here's everything you need to know to start finding profitable real estate deals in Lakewood CO.
author: AIWholesail Team
tags: real estate deals in Lakewood CO
metaDescription: Find profitable real estate deals in Lakewood CO. Market overview, top neighborhoods, average prices, and tips for investors and wholesalers in 2024.
metaKeywords: real estate deals in Lakewood CO, Lakewood Colorado real estate investing, wholesale deals Lakewood CO, investment properties Lakewood Colorado, Denver metro real estate deals, flip houses Lakewood CO, rental properties Lakewood Colorado

sections.content: Tucked right along the western edge of Denver, Lakewood, Colorado doesn't always make the headlines the way its bigger neighbor does. But for savvy real estate investors, that's exactly the point. While everyone fights over deals in Cherry Creek or the Highlands, Lakewood quietly delivers solid returns across wholesaling, flipping, and long-term rentals. If you're serious about finding real estate deals in Lakewood CO, this guide breaks down everything — the local market, the best neighborhoods, the types of deals available, and how to compete whether you're local or investing from out of state. If you're newer to the game, start with our Real Estate Investing for Beginners guide before diving in.
Lakewood CO Market Overview: Why Investors Are Paying Attention

Lakewood is Jefferson County's largest city with roughly 160,000 residents. It sits just 8 miles west of downtown Denver, with direct light rail access via the W Line and easy highway connections via I-70 and US-6. That proximity to Denver is a major driver of property values and rental demand — workers who can't afford Denver proper consistently look to Lakewood as an affordable alternative without sacrificing commute times.

As of late 2024, the median home price in Lakewood sits around $520,000 to $550,000, which is notably below Denver's median of $600,000 or higher. That price gap creates opportunity. Investors can often find distressed or dated properties in the $380,000 to $450,000 range, renovate them, and either sell or rent at a premium. The city also benefits from strong employment anchors including St. Anthony Hospital, the Denver Federal Center (one of the largest federal office complexes in the country), and proximity to Belmar — a major retail and entertainment hub.
'The best deals are rarely found in the most popular zip codes. The smart money follows infrastructure, employment, and undervalued neighborhoods — and Lakewood checks all three boxes right now.'
Best Neighborhoods for Real Estate Deals in Lakewood CO
Not every pocket of Lakewood performs the same way. Different neighborhoods serve different investment strategies, so knowing where to focus your energy matters enormously.
Belmar and South Lakewood

The area around Belmar, roughly between Alameda Avenue and Mississippi Avenue, is one of the city's most active investment zones. You'll find a mix of 1950s and 1960s ranch-style homes that are prime flip candidates. Many still have original kitchens and bathrooms, which means cosmetic renovations can deliver strong ARV bumps. Comparable renovated homes in this pocket are selling in the $550,000 to $620,000 range, which leaves room for a solid margin when you buy right.
Green Mountain and Applewood

Green Mountain is a more established, higher-income neighborhood in the western part of Lakewood. Homes here are well-kept and typically priced in the $600,000 to $750,000 range. This isn't where you'll find cheap wholesale deals, but it's excellent territory for high-end flips and long-term buy-and-hold properties with strong appreciation. Applewood, just north of Green Mountain, has a loyal homeowner base and steady demand from buyers wanting larger lots and quieter streets.
Edgewater and West Colfax Adjacent

The neighborhoods bordering Edgewater and the West Colfax corridor are where the most aggressive value-add opportunities exist right now. Older homes, some in significant disrepair, are available in the $350,000 to $420,000 range — and the area is gentrifying quickly thanks to its walkability and proximity to Sloan's Lake. For wholesalers looking to assign contracts or investors willing to do heavy rehabs, this sub-market is worth a close look.
Types of Real Estate Deals Available in Lakewood CO
Wholesale Deals

Wholesale deals in Lakewood tend to come from probate situations, tired landlords, and homeowners sitting on 30- to 40-year-old properties who want a fast close. Assignment fees of $15,000 to $35,000 are realistic on a well-structured wholesale deal, especially in the West Colfax and Belmar areas. The key is finding motivated sellers before they list on the MLS — which means direct mail, driving for dollars, and networking with local probate attorneys. Our guide on How to Find Off-Market Real Estate Deals walks through the exact strategies that work in competitive suburban markets like Lakewood. If you're newer to the wholesale side, the Wholesaling Real Estate for Beginners guide is a great starting point.
Fix and Flip

Lakewood is a strong flip market. The combination of older housing stock, strong buyer demand from Denver spillover, and appreciating ARVs creates favorable conditions. A typical light-to-medium cosmetic flip in the Belmar area — new kitchen, updated baths, paint, flooring — can cost $50,000 to $80,000 in renovation and still yield a 15% to 20% gross margin. Before you run numbers on any deal, use our free ARV Calculator to quickly estimate after-repair value based on local comps, and pair that with our Deal Calculator to confirm your numbers hold up.
Long-Term Rentals

Rental demand in Lakewood is steady and strong. Average rents for a 3-bedroom single-family home run between $2,100 and $2,600 per month depending on the neighborhood and condition. With interest rates elevated, many potential buyers are staying renters longer — which keeps vacancy rates low. Gross rent multipliers in Lakewood currently run around 14x to 17x, which is competitive but workable if you're buying off-market or at a discount.

Pro Tip: When analyzing rental deals in Lakewood, factor in Jefferson County property taxes (roughly 0.5% to 0.6% effective rate) and don't overlook HOA fees if you're buying in a townhome or condo community near Belmar. These costs can quietly eat into your cash flow if you don't account for them upfront.
Lakewood Real Estate Market Trends to Watch in 2024 and Beyond

items: Light rail expansion continues to increase walkability scores and rental premiums along the W Line corridor
Belmar redevelopment is driving retail and restaurant growth, boosting nearby home values
Inventory remains historically low in Jefferson County, keeping days-on-market short for renovated properties
Remote work trends are sustaining out-of-state buyer interest in Denver metro suburbs like Lakewood
City of Lakewood zoning updates are opening ADU (accessory dwelling unit) opportunities, which can increase cash flow on single-family rentals
Rising insurance costs in Colorado are a headwind — budget 20% to 30% higher than national averages for property insurance
New construction is limited in Lakewood due to infill constraints, keeping pressure on existing home prices

sections.content: Tips for Out-of-State Investors Looking at the Lakewood Market

Remote investing in Lakewood is very doable — but it requires building the right local team before you commit capital. The Denver metro is a fast-moving market. Properties that are well-priced move in days, not weeks. If you're investing from out of state, here's how to set yourself up for success.

items: Build your team first: Find a Lakewood-area real estate agent who works with investors, a local property manager with Jefferson County experience, and a contractor who knows the renovation costs in this specific market
Understand the micro-markets: Lakewood is not one homogenous market — a home in Green Mountain and a home near West Colfax will have very different buyer pools, renovation needs, and ARVs

Use data tools to pre-screen deals remotely: Platforms like AIWholesail let you identify distressed and off-market opportunities in Lakewood before ever booking a flight — visit aiwholesail.com/markets/lakewood-co to explore active deal data for the area
Run conservative numbers: Factor in 10% management fees, 5% vacancy, and a 10% to 15% renovation contingency buffer for every deal you underwrite remotely
Visit before you close: If at all possible, visit the property and neighborhood in person at least once before closing — photos lie, and block-by-block quality differences in Lakewood are significant
Learn to analyze deals quickly: Our guide on How to Analyze Real Estate Deals Like a Pro covers the exact framework to evaluate any deal in Lakewood or any other market

sections.content: 'In competitive suburban markets, the investors who win aren't the ones with the most capital — they're the ones with the best data and the strongest local networks.'
Is Lakewood CO Right for Your Investment Strategy?

Lakewood offers something for almost every type of real estate investor. Wholesalers can find motivated sellers in older neighborhoods with aging housing stock. Flippers have a deep buyer pool and appreciating ARVs to work with. Landlords benefit from stable employment, strong rental demand, and limited new supply. And out-of-state investors can compete effectively with the right tools and local team in place. The key, as always, is buying right, knowing your numbers, and moving quickly when a real deal surfaces.
